commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Rising up from his bed, on which he lay, on account of his great age and weakness, while he addressed himself to his son; (3 Kings i. 47.; Calmet) or he rose from his throne, to honor this grand assembly. Hebrew, "stood upon his feet. "(Haydock)
Thought. Hebrew, "I, with my heart, thought to "(Calmet)
And, or which is the footstool. (Haydock)
The ark is often so called, because God was considered as sitting upon the wings of the cherubim, over it, Psalm xcviii. 1.
Building. No doubt what David left would have been sufficient. Yet Solomon thought that he could make something still more magnificent.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
